chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe phosphate, potassium, and magnesium replacement in refeeding syndrome, emphasizing weight-based dosing, monitoring, and staged calorie advancement in GI disorders.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of phosphate as a priority electrolyte in refeeding syndrome management.
Learn weight-based dosing protocols for phosphate, potassium, and magnesium replacement in pediatric patients.
Recognize the need for monitoring and staged calorie advancement to prevent electrolyte imbalances.
Identify the role of magnesium in correcting hypokalemia and its dosing considerations.
key_facts: Phosphate replacement: 0.2–0.36 mmol/kg per dose IV, with a daily ceiling of 2 mmol/kg/day.
Potassium replacement: Oral dose of 1–2 mmol/kg per dose (max 20 mmol per dose).
Magnesium replacement: Oral dose of 0.1–0.2 mmol/kg per dose, given 2–3 times daily.
Severe hypophosphatemia can cause respiratory failure and arrhythmias during calorie advancement.
Intravenous electrolyte replacement requires cardiac monitoring and adherence to local protocols.
Electrolyte levels should be rechecked before advancing calories after replacement therapy.
reference_quote: Treat severe hypophosphatemia as an emergency because it can precipitate respiratory failure and arrhythmias during calorie advancement.
document_key_topics: Potassium and magnesium dosing in GI disorders
